question.text: Which of the following can be an angel investor in an Angel Fund?

answer.text: An individual with net tangible assets of at least Rs 2 crores (excluding principal residence) with early-stage investment experience
Only institutional investors like banks and insurance companies
Any individual with annual income of at least Rs 50 lakhs
Any retail investor with a minimum investment of Rs 10 lakhs

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An angel investor must be an individual with net tangible assets of at least Rs 2 crores (excluding principal residence) and either early-stage investment experience, serial entrepreneur experience, or 10+ years senior management experience. **The Trap:** There is no Rs 50 lakh income threshold for angel investors. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Angel investors can also be body corporates (Rs 10 crore net worth) or other AIFs/VCFs.
